name: bfsCallers
jsdoc:
/**
 * Performs BFS traversal over the inverted call graph (calledBy) starting
 * from the target symbol, collecting all reachable callers up to `maxDepth`
 * levels. Tracks visited symbols to prevent infinite loops on circular calls.
 */

LanguageAdapter
jsdoc:
/**
 * Language adapter interface. Each supported language provides an implementation
 * that handles symbol extraction, dependency extraction, and call graph building.
 *
 * Adapters produce the same SymbolEntry[] / ImportInfo[] output regardless of
 * language, so the orchestration layer can treat all languages uniformly.
 */

 * Looks for triple-quoted strings immediately following class/def lines.
 */

 * behind the LanguageAdapter interface. Stateless — no initialization needed.

/**

name: rebuildCallGraphForFile
jsdoc:
/**
 * Rebuilds call graph entries for a single changed file, then inverts the full graph.
 * More efficient than rebuilding the entire call graph for incremental updates.
 */

name: rebuildCallGraphForFiles
jsdoc:
/**
 * Rebuilds call graph entries for multiple changed files without inverting.
 * Used by batch processing to avoid cross-file staleness: all files are processed
 * against the same symbol set, then a single invertCallGraph pass is done by the caller.
 * Does not mutate the input array.
 */

name: invertCallGraph
jsdoc:
/**
 * Inverts the call graph to populate the 'calledBy' array for each symbol.
 * Does not mutate the input array.
 */

name: countComplexity
jsdoc:
/**
 * Count cyclomatic complexity of a given AST subtree.
 * Base complexity = 1, plus 1 for each branch point.
 */

name: computeFileComplexity
jsdoc:
/**
 * Compute cyclomatic complexity for all function/method symbols in a source file
 * and attach the result to the symbol entries.
 *
 * Returns the aggregate complexity score for the file (sum of all function complexities).
 */

jsdoc:
/**

name: makeFileContext

name: handleFileChange
jsdoc:
/**
 * Re-processes a single changed file: removes old symbol entries, re-extracts symbols,
 * rebuilds call graph entries, updates summaries cache, and writes both artifacts atomically.
 * Must complete in under 1 second for a single file.
 */

name: handleBatchFileChanges
jsdoc:
/**
 * Processes multiple interdependent file changes in a single pass to avoid cross-file
 * call graph staleness. When files A and B both change in one batch, this ensures that
 * A's call graph is rebuilt against B's fresh symbols (and vice versa), then a single
 * invertCallGraph pass produces consistent calledBy edges.
 */

 */

resolveEnclosingDef
jsdoc:
/**
 * Determines the enclosing function/method scope for a given line based on
 * Python indentation. Returns the qualified name of the enclosing def, or
 * null if the line is at module (top-level) scope.
 *
 * `defStack` is mutated: stale entries whose indentation is >= the current
 * line's indentation are popped before the lookup.
 */

name: addCall
signature: function addCall(caller: SymbolEntry, calleeQualifiedName: string): void
jsdoc:
/**
 * Adds `calleeQualifiedName` to `caller.calls` if not already present.
 * Uses a parallel Set for O(1) membership checks instead of Array.includes().
 */

name: buildPythonCallGraph
jsdoc:
/**
 * Builds a regex-based call graph for Python symbols.
 *
 * This is intentionally imprecise — it captures most direct calls but won't
 * catch aliased or dynamic calls. The caller (build-knowledge.ts) is expected
 * to run `invertCallGraph()` on the merged result, so this function only
 * populates `calls` arrays.
 *
 * @param symbols     All known Python SymbolEntry objects (will NOT be mutated).
 * @param fileContents Map of absolute file path -> file source text.
 * @param projectRoot Absolute path to the project root (used for relativising paths).
 * @returns A new array of SymbolEntry objects with `calls` populated.
 */

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description:
All known Python SymbolEntry objects (will NOT be mutated).
 *

Map of absolute file path -> file source text.
 *

Absolute path to the project root (used for relativising paths).
